name: United States

notes: Typical East Coast route; West Coast adds several hours.
Most moves are long-haul and require early travel and housing logistics.

commonRoutes: Highly Skilled Migrant visa — employer-sponsored work visa for skilled professionals
Dutch American Friendship Treaty (DAFT) — allows US entrepreneurs to start businesses in the Netherlands
Partner / family visa — for spouses or partners of EU / Dutch residents
Study visa — for students enrolled in Dutch universities
notes: Many Americans relocate using the DAFT visa or through multinational employer transfers.
Route suitability depends on your situation and sponsor; confirm with official sources.

countrySpecificNotes: US documents may need apostille certification depending on visa route.

shipping.intro: Shipping household goods from the United States typically takes 4–8 weeks by sea freight. Many US movers use temporary housing during the first 1–2 months.
notes: Choose early between luggage-only relocation, shared container, or full container shipping.
Build buffer for transit and customs; temporary housing often overlaps with shipment arrival.
Plan document and customs requirements for household goods in advance.

heroImageAlt: A man at a desk writing in a notebook, with a laptop showing a world map and a relocation route from the United States to the Netherlands; through the window, a Dutch canal with traditional canal houses and a bridge.

seo.title: Moving to the Netherlands from the United States | Visa, Costs & Timeline
seo.description: A practical guide for Americans moving to the Netherlands including visa routes, relocation costs, documents, and planning tools.

faqOverrides.q: Do Americans need a visa to move to the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Yes, for stays longer than 90 days. US citizens need a residence permit. Common routes include the Highly Skilled Migrant visa, DAFT (for entrepreneurs), partner visa, or study visa.
The Dutch American Friendship Treaty allows US citizens to apply for a residence permit to start or run a business in the Netherlands, without a Dutch employer sponsor.

faqOverrides.q: How long can US citizens stay in the Netherlands?
faqOverrides.a: Without a visa, up to 90 days in 180 days (Schengen). For longer stays you need a residence permit via a route such as work, DAFT, study, or family.

faqOverrides.q: How much does relocation from the US cost?
faqOverrides.a: Costs vary by household size, route, and housing. Typical ranges include flights (€400–€900), shipping (€2,000–€6,500), temporary housing (€1,500–€3,000/month), and first-month setup. Use the relocation cost estimator for a personalized range.

faqOverrides.q: Can Americans open Dutch bank accounts?
faqOverrides.a: Yes. After you have a BSN and address, you can open an account with Dutch banks or digital banks that serve residents. Many Americans use international transfer services for the first months.

scenarioOverrides.title: Software engineer relocating from San Francisco
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on Highly Skilled Migrant visa, tech relocation, and cities like Amsterdam or Eindhoven. Plan document prep, housing search, and first-week registration.

scenarioOverrides.title: Entrepreneur using DAFT visa
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on business registration, Dutch bank account setup, and DAFT requirements. Plan for business plan, proof of funds, and municipality registration.

scenarioOverrides.title: Corporate transfer relocation
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on company relocation packages, housing search, and family move logistics. Coordinate with employer on visa, shipping, and temporary housing.

scenarioOverrides.title: Family relocation from the US
scenarioOverrides.summary: Focus on schools, housing, and documentation for all family members. Plan for longer lead time on documents and housing that fits family size.

customNotes: US movers often compare international transfer services, temporary housing, and shipping options.
